# igo Full AI Context

igo provides compact offline and runtime metadata so AI coding tools can inspect a project without reading every source file.

## Entry Files

- `AGENTS.md`: short operational rules for AI agents
- `CLAUDE.md`: framework usage guide
- `docs/ai.md`: metadata contract and AI workflow
- `context7.json`: package metadata for Context7-style consumers

## Commands

```bash
go test ./...
go run ./cmd/igo doctor .
go run ./cmd/igo ai context . --format json
go run ./cmd/igo ai routes .
go run ./cmd/igo ai schemas .
go run ./cmd/igo ai errors
go run ./cmd/igo ai openapi .
go run ./cmd/igo ai prompt . METHOD PATH
go run ./cmd/igo ai workflow
go run ./cmd/igo ai examples
```

## Runtime Endpoints

Enable:

```go
app.RegisterAIRoutes()
```

Endpoints:

- `GET /_ai/routes`: route metadata
- `GET /_ai/schemas`: type schemas
- `GET /_ai/errors`: framework error code table
- `GET /_ai/info`: framework summary
- `GET /_ai/openapi`: OpenAPI 3.0 JSON
- `GET /_ai/conventions`: workflow and rules
- `GET /_ai/middlewares`: global and route middleware names/order

## Metadata Models

Route metadata uses `core/route.RouteConfig`.

Schema metadata uses `types.TypeSchema` and includes field names, JSON names, Go types, JSON types, validate tags, required flags, enum/min/max/gte/lte/len constraints, descriptions, defaults, and examples.

OpenAPI generation uses both models and writes `components.schemas`. Unknown dynamic response values are represented as generic objects instead of unresolved `$ref` values.

## Handler Conventions

```go
req, ok := igo.BindAndValidate[CreateUserRequest](c)
if !ok {
	return
}
```

```go
q, ok := igo.BindQueryAndValidate[ListQuery](c)
if !ok {
	return
}
```

```go
p, ok := igo.BindPathAndValidate[PathParams](c)
if !ok {
	return
}
```

Use wrap helpers in error branches:

```go
if err != nil {
	c.InternalErrorWrap(err, "failed to create user", map[string]any{"email": req.Email})
	return
}
```

Register response-only DTOs:

```go
app.RegisterSchema(UserResponse{})
igo.RegisterAppSchema[UserResponse](app)
```

Legacy global registration remains available as `igo.RegisterSchema[T]()` but is not recommended for multi-App tests or services.
